brand.name: Charm City Family
brand.tagline: A safe place for local parents and families to celebrate our differences thru support & share joys of living in Baltimore.

offer.what: Hyperlocal Baltimore family community and blog — curated local event recommendations, kid-friendly activity guides, community discussion, and occasional creative services (e.g. caricature art). Primary distribution via Facebook page and group.
offer.coreOutcome: Baltimore parents discover fun, inclusive things to do with their kids and feel connected to a supportive local parenting community.
offer.pricePoint: Free community / no stated paid tier (donation or sponsorship revenue model likely; creative services priced separately)
offer.commitment: No formal commitment — free to join/follow

audience.icp: Baltimore City parents (primarily moms) with children ages 0–12, looking for vetted local activities, family-friendly events, and peer community support
painPoints: Hard to find up-to-date, trustworthy info on family-friendly Baltimore events
Feeling isolated or lacking a local parenting support network
Not knowing which neighborhoods/venues are safe and welcoming for diverse families
segments: New or expectant Baltimore parents seeking community
Established Baltimore families wanting curated weekend plans
Diverse families specifically seeking inclusive, affirming spaces

salesCycle.trial: N/A — free community; no paid trial structure identified
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Organic social follow / Facebook group join; word-of-mouth and algorithmic reach
salesCycle.topObjection: "Is this active / up to date?" — domain is parked and Facebook has modest follower counts (150 page, 361 group members), raising credibility concerns for new visitors

ahaMoment: A new follower sees a post about a local event or activity they didn't know about, goes with their family, and reports back to the group — converting passive follower to active community member.

retention.recurring: Ongoing community membership (Facebook group/page follows); no paid recurring product identified
retention.expansion: Potential: sponsored posts, event partnerships, paid newsletters, or local brand collaborations; creative services (caricatures) as add-on revenue
retention.churnRisk: Low posting cadence / domain inactivity can trigger audience disengagement; algorithm de-prioritizes low-engagement pages

lifecycleGaps: No functioning website or email list — zero owned-channel audience
No welcome/onboarding sequence for new followers
No lead-magnet nurture path to convert social followers to email subscribers
No consistent newsletter or value email cadence
No win-back or re-engagement flow for lapsed community members
No referral or advocacy program to grow the community organically
toneVoice: Casual, warm, and community-first. Uses conversational language and light emoji. Celebrates Baltimore's diversity. Feels like a knowledgeable neighbor sharing a tip, not a brand broadcasting.
compliance: CAN-SPAM / standard email compliance applies. No HIPAA, financial, or legal compliance needs identified. Content involves minors so standard child-safe practices apply.
